description: Python optimal control package for NMR spin dynamics implementing analytical ROPE/CROP theories and numerical GRAPE optimization, with Spinach-compatible API, ensemble support, and paper-reproduction examples.

userStories.title: Create package directory structure and pyproject.toml
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the project scaffold in place so that the package can be installed and developed locally.
acceptanceCriteria: Create directories: optimalcontrol/, tests/, examples/, docs/
Create pyproject.toml with package name 'optimalcontrol', version '0.1.0', and runtime dependencies numpy and scipy
Add dev dependency group with pytest, ruff, mypy, and matplotlib
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Used setuptools build backend (hatchling not available). Added setuptools.packages.find to restrict to optimalcontrol package only.

userStories.title: Create documentation reference files
userStories.description: As a developer, I want source-traceability documents so that every implementation decision can be traced back to the JMR/PNAS papers or Spinach.
acceptanceCriteria: Create docs/source_notes.md summarising what each PDF (JMR 2003 ROPE, PNAS 2003 CROP) contributes to the implementation
Create docs/spinach_mapping.md mapping Spinach MATLAB concepts (grape_xy, control struct, drift/control operators) to their Python module equivalents
Create docs/equations.md documenting ROPE/CROP notation conventions including all symbols used in plan.md Phases 5 and 6
Define and document Hz vs rad/s unit policy: public API uses Hz; internal rad/s conversions occur at documented boundaries
Define and document state normalisation policy: Hilbert-Schmidt norm at all fidelity evaluation points
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Created docs/source_notes.md, docs/spinach_mapping.md, docs/equations.md. Documented Hz vs rad/s unit policy (public API uses Hz; internal rad/s at Hamiltonian builders). Documented Hilbert-Schmidt norm state normalisation policy. Documented Liouville-space column-major vectorisation convention.

userStories.title: Create package __init__.py, README, and smoke test
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a minimal importable package with a README and a passing import test so that CI has a green baseline.
acceptanceCriteria: Create optimalcontrol/__init__.py with __version__ = '0.1.0' and public API placeholders as empty stubs or TODO comments
Create README.md describing project goal, source references (both PDFs, Spinach wiki URL), and local commands: lint (ruff check .), typecheck (mypy optimalcontrol), test (pytest)
Create tests/test_imports.py that imports optimalcontrol and asserts __version__ == '0.1.0'
pytest tests/test_imports.py passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Updated __init__.py with public API placeholder comments (TODO stubs). Created README.md with project goal, PDF references, Spinach wiki URL, and dev commands. Created tests/test_imports.py asserting __version__ == '0.1.0'. Note: bare `pytest` command fails due to editable install path issue in this env; use `python3.10 -m pytest` or `python3 -m pytest` instead.

userStories.title: Implement spin-1/2 single-spin operators
userStories.description: As a developer building spin system models, I need the six elementary spin-1/2 operators so that all Hamiltonians can be constructed from first principles.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement optimalcontrol/operators.py with functions Ix(), Iy(), Iz(), Ip(), Im(), E() each returning a 2x2 numpy.ndarray
Operators satisfy standard spin-1/2 algebra: [Ix,Iy]=i*Iz/2 (cyclic), Ip = Ix+i*Iy, Im = Ix-i*Iy
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented Ix, Iy, Iz, Ip, Im, E in optimalcontrol/operators.py using numpy.typing.NDArray[np.complex128]. Standard relations: [Ix,Iy]=i*Iz (not i*Iz/2 as PRD text states — PRD text is wrong; the actual standard result is i*Iz for spin-1/2 operators I=sigma/2). Ip=Ix+i*Iy, Im=Ix-i*Iy verified.

userStories.title: Implement tensor-product and operator placement helpers
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to build multi-spin operators from single-spin ones so that I can construct two-spin Hamiltonians like 2IzSz.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement kron_product(ops: list) -> ndarray that takes a list of single-spin matrices and returns their Kronecker product
Implement place_operator(op, spin_index, n_spins) -> ndarray that places a single-spin operator at position spin_index in an n_spins system, tensoring with identity elsewhere
Implement two_spin_product(op_i, op_s, n_spins=2) -> ndarray returning op_i ⊗ op_s for a two-spin system
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented kron_product, place_operator, two_spin_product in operators.py. two_spin_product uses place_operator at positions 0 and 1 and matrix-multiplies, which equals np.kron(op_i, op_s) for the 2-spin default. kron_product raises ValueError on empty list.

userStories.title: Implement Hilbert-space commutator and Liouville vectorisation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need commutator and Liouville-space utilities so that I can build superoperators from Hamiltonians.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement comm(A, B) -> ndarray returning A @ B - B @ A
Implement vec(rho) -> ndarray that column-stacks a density matrix into a vector (row-major or column-major, documented in docstring)
Implement unvec(v, dim) -> ndarray that inverts vec()
vec/unvec round-trip: unvec(vec(rho), dim) == rho for arbitrary 2x2 and 4x4 matrices
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added comm, vec, unvec to operators.py. vec uses column-major (Fortran) order: vec(rho).flatten(order='F'), unvec inverts via reshape(order='F'). comm returns A@B - B@A. All three verified against known identities and round-trip tests.

userStories.title: Implement superoperators and Lindblad dissipator
userStories.description: As a developer, I need left/right multiplication superoperators, the Liouvillian commutator, double-commutator, and Lindblad builder so that I can construct full relaxation Liouvillians.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement L_op(A) -> ndarray returning left-multiplication superoperator (A ⊗ I in vec convention)
Implement R_op(A) -> ndarray returning right-multiplication superoperator (I ⊗ A^T in vec convention)
Implement liouvillian_comm(A) -> ndarray returning -1j*(L_op(A) - R_op(A))
Implement double_comm(F, rho_vec) -> ndarray computing action of [F,[F,·]] on vectorised rho
Implement lindblad_dissipator(Fk_list, a_kl) -> ndarray building the full Lindblad superoperator from operator list and coefficient matrix
Implement shape and Hermiticity validation helpers: assert_square(A), assert_hermitian(A, tol)
Implement dense_or_sparse(A, sparse=False) returning scipy.sparse.csr_matrix or ndarray based on flag
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented L_op, R_op, liouvillian_comm, double_comm, lindblad_dissipator, assert_square, assert_hermitian, dense_or_sparse in operators.py. Column-major convention: L_op(A)=I⊗A, R_op(A)=A^T⊗I (PRD text describes row-major formulas — corrected to match column-major vec() convention). All verified against direct computation.

userStories.title: Tests for spin operators and tensor-product helpers
userStories.description: As a developer, I want tests for the operator core so that I can catch regressions in the algebraic identities.
acceptanceCriteria: Create tests/test_operators.py
Test commutation relations [Ix,Iy]=i*Iz/2, [Iy,Iz]=i*Ix/2, [Iz,Ix]=i*Iy/2
Test Ip = Ix + i*Iy and Im = Ix - i*Iy
Test place_operator on a two-spin system: place_operator(Iz,0,2) matches expected 4x4 matrix
Test two_spin_product(Iz, Iz) matches expected 4x4 2IzSz/2 matrix
All assertions use numpy.testing.assert_allclose with rtol=1e-12
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Created tests/test_operators.py with 10 tests. Commutation relations verified as [Ix,Iy]=i*Iz (PRD text says /2 which is wrong for I=sigma/2 convention). two_spin_product(Iz,Iz) matches diag(0.25,-0.25,-0.25,0.25). All pass with rtol=1e-12.

userStories.title: Tests for Liouville-space utilities and superoperators
userStories.description: As a developer, I want tests for the Liouville-space layer so that vectorisation and superoperator action are verified against direct computation.
acceptanceCriteria: Add to tests/test_operators.py (or create tests/test_liouville.py)
Test vec/unvec round-trip on 2x2 and 4x4 random Hermitian matrices
Test liouvillian_comm(A) action on vec(rho) matches direct comm(A, rho) vectorised
Test Lindblad dissipator with a single operator F and unit coefficient reproduces expected formula
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Created tests/test_liouville.py with 4 tests: vec/unvec round-trip on 2x2 and 4x4 random Hermitian matrices, liouvillian_comm action vs direct -i*comm, and Lindblad dissipator vs explicit D(rho)=F*rho*F†-0.5*(F†F*rho+rho*F†F). Helper _random_hermitian uses np.random.Generator and returns NDArray[np.complex128]. All tests pass with rtol=1e-12.

userStories.title: Define spin system dataclasses
userStories.description: As a researcher, I want structured dataclasses for spins, couplings, relaxation rates, and spin systems so that I can describe my NMR sample in one place.
acceptanceCriteria: Create optimalcontrol/spin_system.py
Define Spin dataclass: isotope (str), gamma (float, rad/s/T), channel (str)
Define Coupling dataclass: spin_i (int), spin_j (int), J_hz (float)
Define RelaxationRates dataclass: kDD (float), kCSA_I (float), kCSA_S (float), ka (float), kc (float), ka_prime (float), kc_prime (float); all in rad/s
Define SpinSystem dataclass: spins (list[Spin]), couplings (list[Coupling]), shifts_hz (dict[int,float]), relaxation (RelaxationRates), basis (str, default 'dense')
Define two_spin_system(J_hz, kDD, kCSA_I, kCSA_S, ka, kc, ka_prime, kc_prime) factory returning SpinSystem for the paper's I-S heteronuclear system
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Created optimalcontrol/spin_system.py with Spin, Coupling, RelaxationRates, and SpinSystem dataclasses. RelaxationRates stores all fields in rad/s and exposes kI/kS convenience properties. two_spin_system() returns an on-resonance two-spin I-S heteronuclear model with spins 0=I/source and 1=S/target, a single J_hz coupling, zero shifts_hz, and default dense basis.

userStories.title: Implement Hamiltonian builders for the spin system
userStories.description: As a researcher, I want functions that build the J-coupling drift Hamiltonian, chemical-shift Hamiltonian, and control operators from a SpinSystem so that I can assemble the total generator.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement drift_hamiltonian(sys: SpinSystem) -> ndarray building H_J = 2*pi*J * IzSz for each coupling
Implement shift_hamiltonian(sys: SpinSystem) -> ndarray building H_CS = 2*pi * sum(delta_i * Iz_i)
Implement control_operators(sys: SpinSystem) -> dict[str, ndarray] returning {'Ix': ..., 'Iy': ..., 'Sx': ..., 'Sy': ...} for a two-spin system
All operators returned in rad/s units (multiply Hz values by 2*pi at construction)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added drift_hamiltonian(), shift_hamiltonian(), and control_operators() to optimalcontrol/spin_system.py. J couplings and chemical shifts are converted from public Hz fields to rad/s with 2*pi at construction. drift_hamiltonian sums 2*pi*J_hz*Iz_i*Iz_j over couplings with spin-index validation; control_operators returns Ix/Iy on spin 0 and Sx/Sy on spin 1 for two-spin systems.

userStories.title: Implement relaxation Liouvillian builders and total generator
userStories.description: As a researcher, I want relaxation Liouvillian builders (with and without DD-CSA cross-correlation) and a total generator assembler so that I can construct the full propagator.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement relaxation_liouvillian(sys: SpinSystem) -> ndarray building the secular relaxation superoperator without cross-correlation using kI = kDD + kCSA_I and kS = kDD + kCSA_S
Implement relaxation_liouvillian_crosscorr(sys: SpinSystem) -> ndarray building the full Liouvillian including DD-CSA cross-correlation terms ka, kc, ka_prime, kc_prime
Implement total_generator(sys: SpinSystem, controls: dict[str,float]) -> ndarray returning L = L_drift + L_relax + sum(u_k * L_ctrl_k)
Implement validate_onresonance(sys: SpinSystem) raising ValueError if shifts are non-zero when on-resonance assumption is required
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented relaxation_liouvillian(), relaxation_liouvillian_crosscorr(), total_generator(), and validate_onresonance() in optimalcontrol/spin_system.py. Non-cross relaxation uses kI/kS pure-dephasing dissipators; cross-correlated relaxation uses effective ka/ka_prime baselines with kc/kc_prime couplings to the 2IzSz dephasing axis. total_generator assembles drift+shift Liouvillian, relaxation, and rad/s control amplitudes.

userStories.title: Tests for spin system Hamiltonians and relaxation rates
userStories.description: As a developer, I want tests verifying that Hamiltonians and Liouvillians match known analytical expressions for the two-spin paper system.
acceptanceCriteria: Create tests/test_spin_system.py
Test drift_hamiltonian: 2x2 block structure consistent with 2*pi*J*IzSz for a known J
Test control_operators: Ix operator at spin 0 matches place_operator(Ix(), 0, 2)
Test relaxation rates: kI = kDD + kCSA_I, kS = kDD + kCSA_S from RelaxationRates fields
Test cross-correlated rates: ka, kc, ka_prime, kc_prime accessible from RelaxationRates
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Created tests/test_spin_system.py covering two-spin J drift Hamiltonian, Ix control placement on spin 0, kI/kS relaxation properties, and cross-correlated RelaxationRates fields.

userStories.title: Implement product-operator state construction and normalisation
userStories.description: As a researcher, I want to build initial and target states from product-operator labels and normalise them so that fidelity evaluations are correctly scaled.
acceptanceCriteria: Create optimalcontrol/states.py
Implement state_from_label(label: str, n_spins: int) -> ndarray supporting labels: 'Ix','Iy','Iz','Sx','Sy','Sz','2IxSz','2IySz','2IzSx','2IzSy','2IzSz'
Implement single_transition_operator(label: str) -> ndarray supporting labels: 'IzSalpha','IzSbeta','IxSbeta','IySbeta'
Implement normalise_hs(v: ndarray) -> ndarray normalising by Hilbert-Schmidt norm sqrt(Tr(v†v))
Implement normalise_2norm(v: ndarray) -> ndarray normalising by Euclidean 2-norm
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Created optimalcontrol/states.py with product-operator labels for I/S two-spin notation, unnormalised single-transition operators using S alpha/beta projectors, and Hilbert-Schmidt / Euclidean norm helpers. State labels follow I=spin 0 and S=spin 1; product labels such as 2IzSz include the conventional leading factor of 2.

userStories.title: Implement fidelity functions
userStories.description: As a researcher, I want overlap fidelity functions (real, imaginary, absolute-square) and multi-source/target averaged fidelity so that I can measure transfer efficiency for ROPE/CROP and GRAPE.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement fidelity_real(rho_f, rho_t) -> float returning Re(Tr(rho_t† @ rho_f))
Implement fidelity_abs2(rho_f, rho_t) -> float returning |Tr(rho_t† @ rho_f)|^2
Implement fidelity_avg(rho_f_list, rho_t_list, weights=None) -> float returning weighted average fidelity over source-target pairs
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented fidelity_real(), fidelity_imag(), fidelity_abs2(), and fidelity_avg() in optimalcontrol/states.py. Fidelity uses the Hilbert-Schmidt overlap np.vdot(rho_t, rho_f), which matches Tr(rho_t† @ rho_f) for matrix states and also supports vectorised states. Added tests/test_fidelity.py covering self-overlap, complex overlap components, weighted averaging, and shape validation.

userStories.title: Implement prefix, suffix, and dead-time hooks
userStories.description: As a researcher, I want prefix transformation, suffix transformation, and dead-time drift propagation hooks so that I can handle INEPT-style bookend pulses and echo delays around the optimised sequence.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement apply_prefix(rho, prefix_propagator) -> ndarray applying a fixed pre-rotation to the initial state
Implement apply_suffix(rho, suffix_propagator) -> ndarray applying a fixed post-rotation before fidelity evaluation
Implement dead_time_propagation(rho, generator, t_dead) -> ndarray propagating rho under generator for time t_dead using scipy.linalg.expm
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented apply_prefix(), apply_suffix(), and dead_time_propagation() in optimalcontrol/states.py. Matrix states accept Hilbert-space rotations (U @ rho @ U.conj().T) or Liouville-space propagators; vectorised states require Liouville-space propagators. Dead-time propagation uses scipy.linalg.expm(generator * t_dead) and column-major vec/unvec for matrix states.

userStories.title: Tests for states and fidelity
userStories.description: As a developer, I want tests verifying paper transfer fidelities and hook behaviour so that the state/fidelity layer is validated against known results.
acceptanceCriteria: Create tests/test_states.py
Test state_from_label('Iz', 2) returns a 4x4 matrix matching place_operator(Iz(),0,2)
Test fidelity_real(rho, rho) == 1.0 for a normalised state
Test paper transfers produce non-zero fidelity: state_from_label('Iz',2) is non-orthogonal to state_from_label('2IzSz',2)
Test dead_time_propagation with t_dead=0 returns rho unchanged
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Created tests/test_states.py covering Iz state construction, normalised self-fidelity, target-state self-fidelity, and zero-time dead-time propagation. Corrected the direct Iz vs 2IzSz criterion: under the documented Hilbert-Schmidt product-operator convention these states are orthogonal before any transfer propagator, so the regression asserts zero direct overlap and non-zero target self-overlap.

userStories.title: Implement unconstrained ROPE efficiency and INEPT comparison
userStories.description: As a researcher, I want the unconstrained ROPE efficiency formula and INEPT comparison so that I can quantify the gain from optimal control over the standard INEPT sequence.
acceptanceCriteria: Create optimalcontrol/rope.py
Implement rope_n(kI, J) -> float returning n = kI / J (with kI and J in same units, Hz)
Implement rope_g(n) -> float returning g = sqrt(1 + n^2) - n
Implement inept_efficiency(t, J_hz, k_hz) -> float returning exp(-pi*k*t)*sin(pi*J*t)
Implement inept_optimal_time(n, J_hz) -> float returning t* = arccot(n)/(pi*J)
Implement inept_max_efficiency(n, J_hz) -> float evaluating inept_efficiency at t*
Implement rope_gain_over_inept(n, J_hz) -> float returning g / inept_max_efficiency
Implement rope_g_inphase(n) -> float for the in-phase Ix->Sx transfer efficiency (documented formula from JMR paper)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement ROPE optimal return function and trajectory invariant
userStories.description: As a researcher, I want the optimal return function V and the trajectory invariant relation so that I can verify the optimal trajectory condition for any point (r1, r2).
acceptanceCriteria: Implement rope_V(r1, r2, n) -> float returning sqrt(g(n)^2 * r1^2 + r2^2)
Implement rope_u_ratio(r1, r2, n) -> float returning u2/u1 = g*r1/r2 with safe handling when r2==0 (return inf) and r1==0 (return 0)
Implement rope_trajectory_invariant(expect_2IySz, expect_Ix, n) -> float computing the ratio and comparing to g(n) — returns the ratio for the caller to assert
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement finite-time ROPE: critical duration, j(s), switching time solver
userStories.description: As a researcher, I want the finite-time ROPE calculations (Tcrit, Appendix B j(s) function, switching time solver) so that I can compute optimal controls for a constrained transfer duration T.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement rope_Tcrit(n, J_hz) -> float returning T_crit = arccot(2n)/(pi*J)
Implement rope_j(s, n) -> float implementing the j(s) function from Appendix B of JMR 2003
Implement rope_switching_time(T, n, J_hz) -> float solving for switching time s given total duration T using scipy.optimize; raise ValueError if T <= T_crit
Implement rope_finite_angles(T, n, J_hz) -> tuple[float,float] returning (h1, h2)
Implement rope_finite_efficiency(T, n, J_hz) -> float returning g_T
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement finite-time ROPE waveform generator
userStories.description: As a researcher, I want the three-phase ROPE control waveform sampler so that I can obtain time-discretised controls, RF amplitudes, and phases ready for use in a spectrometer.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement rope_phase1_control(t, s, h1, h2, n) -> tuple[float,float] returning (u1(t), u2(t)) for phase I (t in [0, s])
Implement rope_phase3_control(t, T, s, h1, h2, n) -> tuple[float,float] returning controls for phase III using symmetry u2(t) = u1(T-t)
Implement rope_waveform(T, n, J_hz, dt) -> dict with keys 'times', 'u1', 'u2', 'amplitude', 'phase' as numpy arrays sampled at interval dt
Implement rope_hard_pulse_angle(h, amplitude) -> float converting a phase amplitude to a flip angle in degrees
Phase II region (s to T-s) returns u1=u2=1 exactly
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Tests for unconstrained ROPE
userStories.description: As a developer, I want tests for the unconstrained ROPE formulas so that known analytical limits are verified.
acceptanceCriteria: Create tests/test_rope.py
Test rope_g(n=0) == 1.0 (no-relaxation limit)
Test inept_max_efficiency(n=0, J_hz=100) is close to 1.0
Test rope_gain_over_inept(n=0, J_hz=100) == 1.0 within tolerance
All assertions use numpy.testing.assert_allclose with rtol=1e-8
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Tests for finite-time ROPE
userStories.description: As a developer, I want tests for finite-time ROPE calculations so that the switching time solver and efficiency limits are validated.
acceptanceCriteria: Test rope_Tcrit(n=1, J_hz=100) matches expected value arccot(2)/(pi*100)
Test rope_finite_efficiency(T, n=1, J_hz=100) approaches rope_g(n=1) as T -> large (e.g., T = 10/J)
Test rope_waveform returns arrays of correct length ceil(T/dt) and u1==u2==1 in the phase II region
Test that T <= Tcrit raises ValueError from rope_switching_time
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement CROP efficiency and physical transfer limits
userStories.description: As a researcher, I want the CROP analytical efficiency functions and physical transfer limit table so that I can compute decoherence boundaries for cross-correlated spin systems.
acceptanceCriteria: Create optimalcontrol/crop.py
Implement crop_zeta(ka, kc, J_hz) -> float returning sqrt((ka^2-kc^2)/(J^2+kc^2)); raise ValueError if ka < kc
Implement crop_eta(ka, kc, J_hz) -> float returning sqrt(1+zeta^2) - zeta
Implement crop_eta_prime(ka_prime, kc_prime, J_hz) -> float for spin-S rates
Implement crop_limit_Iz_to_2IzSz(eta) -> float returning eta
Implement crop_limit_2IzSz_to_Sz(eta_prime) -> float returning eta_prime
Implement crop_limit_Iz_to_Sz(eta, eta_prime) -> float returning eta * eta_prime
Implement crop_limit_single_transition(eta, eta_prime) -> float returning sqrt(eta^2 + eta_prime^2)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement CROP limiting cases and pulse parameter container
userStories.description: As a researcher, I want the CROP limiting-case functions and a pulse parameter container so that I can verify kc=0 reduces to ROPE and kc/ka->1 approaches lossless transfer.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement crop_kc0_limit(ka, J_hz) -> float verifying that crop_eta(ka, kc=0, J_hz) matches rope_g(n=ka/J_hz) within 1e-10
Implement crop_lossless_limit(ka, J_hz) -> float returning crop_eta(ka, kc=ka, J_hz) which should approach 1.0 as kc->ka
Implement single_transition_decomposition(sys: SpinSystem) -> dict returning {'slowly_relaxing': str, 'fast_relaxing': str} identifying multiplet components
Define CROPPulse dataclass: amplitude (float), irradiation_freq_hz (float), truncation_window (float)
Implement crop_pulse_params(ka, kc, J_hz) -> CROPPulse computing the CROP pulse amplitude and irradiation frequency
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement CROP waveform generator and robustness sweep
userStories.description: As a researcher, I want a truncated CROP waveform generator and a robustness sweep so that I can generate pulse shapes and evaluate how performance varies with relaxation parameters.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement crop_waveform(ka, kc, J_hz, dt, truncation_window) -> dict with keys 'times', 'amplitude', 'irrad_freq' as numpy arrays
Implement crop_robustness_sweep(ka_over_J_values, kc_over_ka_values, J_hz) -> ndarray of shape (len(ka_over_J), len(kc_over_ka)) containing crop_eta values
Values in robustness_sweep are in range [0, 1]; assert no value exceeds 1+1e-10
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Tests for CROP analytical functions
userStories.description: As a developer, I want tests verifying CROP efficiency against the PNAS paper limiting cases and regression values.
acceptanceCriteria: Create tests/test_crop.py
Test crop_kc0_limit: crop_eta(ka=60, kc=0, J_hz=100) matches rope_g(n=0.6) within rtol=1e-8
Test crop_lossless_limit: crop_eta(ka=100, kc=99.999, J_hz=100) is within 0.01 of 1.0
Test monotonic improvement: for kc in [0, 0.3*ka, 0.6*ka, 0.9*ka], crop_eta is non-decreasing
Test regression values: crop_eta(ka=60, kc=45, J_hz=100) and crop_eta(ka=110, kc=82.5, J_hz=100) match stored expected values within rtol=1e-6
Test no efficiency above 1+1e-10 in robustness_sweep over a 5x5 grid
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Define ControlProblem dataclass
userStories.description: As a researcher, I want a ControlProblem dataclass that mirrors the Spinach control structure so that I can describe a GRAPE optimisation problem in familiar terms.
acceptanceCriteria: Create optimalcontrol/grape.py

Define ControlProblem dataclass with required fields: drifts (list[ndarray]), operators (list[ndarray]), rho_init (list[ndarray]), rho_targ (list[ndarray]), pulse_dt (float), pwr_levels (list[float]), freeze (ndarray | None)

Define optional fields: fidelity_mode (str, default 'real'), offsets (list[float] | None), offset_operators (list[ndarray] | None), phase_cycle (ndarray | None), basis (str, default 'dense'), penalties (list | None), checkpoint_path (str | None)
Implement validate_control_problem(cp: ControlProblem) raising ValueError for mismatched dimensions or invalid fidelity_mode
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement waveform shape utilities and forward propagation cache
userStories.description: As a developer, I want waveform validation, freeze-mask application, and a forward propagation cache so that GRAPE has a clean, testable propagation layer.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement validate_waveform(wfm: ndarray, n_channels: int, n_steps: int) raising ValueError for wrong shape
Implement apply_freeze(wfm: ndarray, freeze_mask: ndarray | None) -> ndarray returning wfm with frozen entries held at their initial values
Implement forward_propagators(cp: ControlProblem, wfm: ndarray) -> list[ndarray] returning list of per-slice propagators U_k using scipy.linalg.expm
Implement forward_states(rho_init: ndarray, propagators: list[ndarray]) -> list[ndarray] returning accumulated states [rho_0, rho_1, ..., rho_N]
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement backward adjoint propagation and grape_xy fidelity
userStories.description: As a developer, I want backward adjoint propagation and the grape_xy fidelity entry point so that the core GRAPE loop is functional.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement backward_states(rho_targ: ndarray, propagators: list[ndarray]) -> list[ndarray] returning backward-propagated target states
Implement final_fidelity(fwd_states: list, bwd_states: list, mode: str) -> float evaluating fidelity between final forward state and initial backward state
Implement grape_xy(cp: ControlProblem, wfm: ndarray) -> float returning scalar fidelity (Spinach-compatible entry point, single return value)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement directional derivative and gradient accumulation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the auxiliary-matrix directional derivative of the matrix exponential and gradient accumulation over slices so that GRAPE has an analytical gradient.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement dir_diff_expm(H, dH, dt) -> ndarray computing the directional derivative of expm(-1j*H*dt) in direction dH using the 2x2 block auxiliary matrix method
Implement grape_gradient(cp: ControlProblem, wfm: ndarray) -> ndarray of same shape as wfm containing dF/d(wfm[k,c]) accumulated over slices and control channels
Gradient entries for frozen channels (freeze_mask==True) are set to zero
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement Hessian path and Liouville/Hilbert GRAPE variants
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a Hessian placeholder, an exact Hessian for small systems, and Liouville/Hilbert-space variant dispatch so that GRAPE can be used in different physical models.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement grape_hessian(cp: ControlProblem, wfm: ndarray) -> ndarray | None returning None when n_steps * n_channels > 50 (Hessian too large) or the exact Hessian matrix otherwise
Implement grape_xy_liouville(cp: ControlProblem, wfm: ndarray) -> float for Liouville-space propagation (density matrix in vectorised form)
Implement grape_xy_hilbert(cp: ControlProblem, wfm: ndarray) -> float for Hilbert-space propagation (pure-state, closed system)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement phase-only and amplitude-phase GRAPE adapters
userStories.description: As a researcher, I want phase-only and amplitude-phase parameterisation adapters so that I can optimise in amplitude/phase space and convert back to Cartesian XY.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement xy_to_ampl_phase(wfm_xy: ndarray) -> tuple[ndarray, ndarray] returning (amplitude, phase) arrays
Implement ampl_phase_to_xy(amplitude: ndarray, phase: ndarray) -> ndarray returning Cartesian wfm
Implement phase_only_gradient(grad_xy: ndarray, amplitude: ndarray) -> ndarray converting Cartesian gradient to phase-only gradient via chain rule
Implement curvilinear_reparameterise(wfm: ndarray, bounds: tuple[float,float]) -> ndarray applying tanh reparameterisation to enforce bounds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement ensemble expansion over drift generators and power levels

Implement expand_power_levels(cp: ControlProblem) -> list[ControlProblem] returning one problem per pwr_levels entry with scaled operators
Implement ensemble_fidelity(cp: ControlProblem, wfm: ndarray) -> float returning mean fidelity over all expanded problems
Implement ensemble_gradient(cp: ControlProblem, wfm: ndarray) -> ndarray returning mean gradient over all expanded problems

Implement correlated_rho_match(cp: ControlProblem) -> list[ControlProblem] for rho_match mode

Implement joblib_backend(fn, problems: list, n_jobs=-1) -> list applying fn in parallel using joblib if available, falling back to serial otherwise

userStories.title: Implement waveform penalty functions
userStories.description: As a researcher, I want norm-square and derivative norm-square penalty functions with values, gradients, and separated reporting so that hardware constraints can be included in optimisation.
acceptanceCriteria: Create optimalcontrol/penalties.py
Implement penalty_NS(wfm, weight) -> tuple[float, ndarray] returning (value, gradient) for norm-square penalty
Implement penalty_SNS(wfm, limit, weight) -> tuple[float, ndarray] for spillout norm-square Cartesian penalty (energy above limit)
Implement penalty_SNSA(wfm, limit, weight) -> tuple[float, ndarray] for spillout norm-square amplitude penalty
Implement penalty_DNS(wfm, weight) -> tuple[float, ndarray] for derivative norm-square penalty (finite differences of wfm along time axis)
Implement total_penalty(wfm, penalty_list) -> tuple[float, ndarray] summing all active penalties
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement waveform distortion models
userStories.description: As a researcher, I want amplifier and filter distortion models with derivative hooks so that GRAPE gradients remain correct when hardware non-idealities are included.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement distortion_noop(wfm) -> ndarray returning wfm unchanged
Implement distortion_tanh(wfm, scale) -> ndarray applying tanh(wfm/scale)*scale compression
Implement distortion_root(wfm, scale) -> ndarray applying sign(wfm)*sqrt(|wfm|/scale)*scale
Implement distortion_single_pole(wfm, alpha) -> ndarray applying IIR low-pass filter with pole alpha
Implement distortion_single_zero(wfm, beta) -> ndarray applying FIR zero filter with coefficient beta
Each distortion function has a companion distortion_*_deriv(wfm, ...) -> ndarray returning element-wise derivative for gradient chain rule
Implement lower_upper_clip(wfm, lb, ub) -> ndarray clipping for diagnostics only (not used in gradient path)
Typecheck passes

Test penalty_DNS gradient against finite differences with rtol=1e-6

userStories.title: Implement gradient ascent and LBFGS optimizers

Implement lbfgs_update(state, wfm_diff, grad_diff) -> dict updating the LBFGS memory
Implement lbfgs_direction(state, grad) -> ndarray returning the LBFGS quasi-Newton search direction
Implement lbfgs_grape(cp, wfm0, m=10, tol_x=1e-6, tol_g=1e-6, max_iter=500) -> OptimResult

userStories.title: Implement Newton-Raphson optimizer with convergence checks
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a Newton-Raphson optimizer with Hessian regularisation and RFO option so that small-waveform problems can converge quadratically.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement newton_raphson(cp, wfm0, regularise=True, rfo=False, tol_x=1e-6, tol_g=1e-6, max_iter=200) -> OptimResult
Regularisation adds lambda*I to the Hessian when it is indefinite (lambda chosen by minimum eigenvalue + epsilon)
RFO option applies the rational function optimisation level-shifted step when rfo=True
Convergence checks: step norm < tol_x OR gradient norm < tol_g OR n_iter >= max_iter
OptimResult.reason is one of 'step_tol', 'grad_tol', 'max_iter'
Typecheck passes

All three optimizers (gradient_ascent, lbfgs_grape, newton_raphson) accept checkpoint_path: str | None; if provided, save checkpoint every 10 iterations and load on start if file exists
